Wikimedia Commons has media related to [2020 European Figure Skating Championships](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/Category:2020_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "commons:Category:2020 European Figure Skating Championships").

The **2020 [European Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/European_Figure_Skating_Championships "European Figure Skating Championships")** were held in [Graz, Austria](/wiki/Graz,_Austria "Graz, Austria"), on 20–26 January 2020.[[1]](#cite_note-host-1) Medals were awarded in the disciplines of [men's singles, ladies' singles](/wiki/Single_skating "Single skating"), [pairs](/wiki/Pair_skating "Pair skating"), and [ice dance](/wiki/Ice_dance "Ice dance"). The competition determined the entry quotas for each federation at the [2021 European Championships](/wiki/2021_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2021 European Figure Skating Championships").

## Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2020_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Qualification")]

### Age and minimum TES requirements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2020_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Age and minimum TES requirements")]

The competition is open to skaters from all European [member nations](/wiki/International_figure_skating "International figure skating") of the [International Skating Union](/wiki/International_Skating_Union "International Skating Union"). The corresponding competition for non-European skaters is the [2020 Four Continents Figure Skating Championships](/wiki/2020_Four_Continents_Figure_Skating_Championships "2020 Four Continents Figure Skating Championships").

Skaters are eligible for the 2020 European Championships if they turned 15 years of age before 1 July 2019 and have met the minimum technical elements score requirements. The ISU accepts scores if they were obtained at senior-level ISU-recognized international competitions at least 21 days before the first official practice day of the championships.[[1]](#cite_note-host-1)

### Number of entries per discipline

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2020_European_Figure_Skating_Championships&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Number of entries per discipline")]

Based on the results of the [2019 European Championships](/wiki/2019_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2019 European Figure Skating Championships"), each country can field one to three entries per discipline.[[2]](#cite_note-n_entries-2)
